## Analysis Complete

Here's what I found:

### The Bug — `src/utils.ts`, line 7

```typescript
// ❌ Current (broken)
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {

// ✅ Fix
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
```

The loop condition `i < items.length - 1` stops **one iteration early**, so the last element is always skipped. Removing `- 1` is the entire fix.

### Impact

| Input | Current (broken) output | Expected output |
|-------|------------------------|-----------------|
| `["hello", "world", "foo"]` | `["HELLO", "WORLD"]` | `["HELLO", "WORLD", "FOO"]` |
| `["test"]` | `[]` | `["TEST"]` |
| `["a", "b"]` | `["A"]` | `["A", "B"]` |

### Tests

All 4 `processItems` tests in `tests/utils.test.ts` already cover this bug correctly — they'll go from failing → passing with the fix. No test changes needed.

### Verification

```bash
npx vitest run  # all 8 tests should pass
```

Approve the plan above to proceed with the one-line fix.
